cases.input: Design a GitHub Actions pipeline to deploy a .NET API to Azure App Service. We need secure Azure authentication without storing secrets, and the pipeline should run for PRs and main branch separately.
cases.context: GitHub-hosted org, Azure subscription, no compliance gates required

cases.input: We deploy 8 microservices to AKS across dev, staging, and production environments. We want GitOps-based deployments where a Git commit automatically syncs to the cluster. What approach should we use?
cases.context: AKS cluster already running, GitHub as SCM, team prefers Azure-native tools

cases.input: We want to roll out our new Container Apps revision progressively - starting with 1% of traffic, then 10%, 50%, and finally 100%. How do we implement ring deployments with automatic health gates?
cases.context: Azure Container Apps, stateless HTTP service, GitHub Actions for CI

cases.input: Our team pushes Terraform changes and applies them locally to production. We need to enforce that: plan is shown in PR comments, apply only happens after merge, and no direct local applies to shared environments.
cases.context: GitHub org, Terraform for Azure infrastructure, 5-person DevOps team

cases.input: We want to enforce Conventional Commits in all PRs and set up Dependabot to auto-merge minor/patch dependency updates when tests pass. How should we configure this?
cases.context: GitHub repository with NuGet, npm, and GitHub Actions dependencies
